ip_outcome_note: Card name directly names the depicted character. Confirmed via Bandai official listing, Saiyan/World Tournament traits, and Majin Buu Saga era stamp. Frontmatter updated: ip_verified: true.

trivia_bullets.text: TB2-025 is from World Martial Arts Tournament (DBS-TB02), released September 21, 2018 — final set in the Over Realm block. TB2 introduced the Link Cards mechanic: paired cards with correlating artworks whose skills activate only when the matched partner is in play. TB2-025's partner is Begrudging Respect Piccolo (TB2-027). Blue cards in TB2 represent the 25th World Tournament / Majin Buu Saga era.
sources: dbs-cardgame.com: World Martial Arts Tournament Design Concept strategy page
cardmarket.com: World Martial Arts Tournament TB2 set entry

trivia_bullets.text: Blue Battle card, Uncommon, power 20,000, energy cost 4. Traits: Saiyan/World Tournament. Permanent Barrier (cannot be chosen by opponent's skills). Auto when played: if Leader is World Tournament and Begrudging Respect Piccolo is in your Battle Area, opponent places 2 hand cards on top of deck in any order. Linked partner Piccolo gains power boost and energy-disruption capability when Vegeta is in play — a mutual two-card hand-and-energy disruption engine.

The 'Begrudging Respect' subtitle references the canonical Buu Saga moment before Vegeta's self-sacrifice: Piccolo most fully understood that Vegeta's sacrifice was both selfish and selfless. Piccolo had already killed Babidi by this point; Vegeta's witnessing of Piccolo's triumphant act of self-destruction is analyzed as a trigger for Vegeta's own decision to detonate himself against Buu. The arms-crossed, closed-off posture (per Vision section) encodes Vegeta's canonical inability to openly acknowledge non-Saiyan respect.
sources: CBR: 'Vegeta and Piccolo Never Fought in DBZ'
Quora: 'When did Piccolo earn Vegeta's respect?'

trivia_bullets.text: TB2-025 completes the final unenriched slot in the Vegeta character node corpus roster (per _characters/vegeta.md wave 117 note). As the corpus's second Buu-Saga-phase Vegeta print alongside TB2-006 Vegeta Power Misused, it provides the contrasting Buu-Saga emotional register: the quietly-processing Vegeta about to sacrifice himself vs. the Majin-unleashed aggressor of TB2-006.
